issues.description: Both endpoints (PATCH /erpHrm/members and GET /erpHrm/members/:memberId) are marked with '@x-autobe-authorization-type null' and have no authentication guard applied. The member list endpoint exposes organization member data including emails, phone numbers, and avatars without any authentication. The spec mentions organization-level data isolation but no guard enforces this at the controller level.
issues.suggestion: Apply an appropriate authentication guard (AdminAuth or MemberAuth decorator) to both endpoints to ensure only authenticated users within the organization can access member data.

issues.description: All controllers use console.log(error) for error handling. This is the standard AutoBE pattern but lacks structured logging with request context, user identity, and severity levels.
issues.suggestion: Consider implementing a structured logging solution (e.g., Winston, Pino) with request correlation IDs and user context for better observability and incident response.

issues.description: No rate limiting is applied to any endpoint across the codebase. This applies to all controllers in this chunk.
issues.suggestion: Implement rate limiting at the API gateway or using NestJS ThrottlerModule, especially for search/list endpoints that could be abused for data harvesting.

issues.description: Password complexity validation is not enforced in the member management endpoints. This is a known AutoBE limitation.
issues.suggestion: Add password complexity requirements (minimum length, character classes) when creating or updating member accounts.

issues.description: The provider fetches any organization by ID without verifying that the requesting admin belongs to or has authority over that organization. An admin could enumerate and access any organization's data by supplying arbitrary UUIDs.
issues.suggestion: Add an organization ownership/membership check: verify that the admin's organization context matches the requested organizationId before returning data. E.g., add `erp_hrm_admin_id: props.admin.id` or an organization membership filter to the where clause.

issues.description: The report deletion provider fetches and deletes a report by ID without verifying that the report belongs to the admin's organization. Any admin can delete any report across all organizations by supplying an arbitrary reportId.
issues.suggestion: Add an organization scope filter to the findUniqueOrThrow query: `where: { id: props.reportId, erp_hrm_organization_id: props.admin.organizationId }` to ensure cross-organization deletion is prevented.

issues.description: The report parameters update provider fetches the report by ID only (`where: { id: props.reportId }`) without verifying organization ownership. An admin from one organization can modify report parameters belonging to another organization.
issues.suggestion: Add organization context to the initial report lookup: `where: { id: props.reportId, erp_hrm_organization_id: props.admin.organizationId }` and to all subsequent queries.

issues.description: The bulk permission update provider fetches a role by ID without verifying that the role belongs to the admin's organization. An admin could modify permissions of roles in other organizations.
issues.suggestion: Add organization scope to the role lookup: `where: { id: props.roleId, erp_hrm_organization_id: props.admin.organizationId }` to prevent cross-organization role permission modification.

issues.description: The project member update provider validates that the project exists and the member belongs to the project, but does not verify that the project belongs to the admin's organization. An admin could update project members in other organizations.
issues.suggestion: Add organization scope to the project lookup: `where: { id: props.projectId, erp_hrm_organization_id: props.admin.organizationId }` to enforce organization isolation.

issues.description: Role name uniqueness is checked globally across all organizations instead of being scoped to the admin's organization. The comment even acknowledges this: 'we perform global uniqueness check across all organizations'. This means a role name used in any other organization will incorrectly block creation in the current organization.
issues.suggestion: Scope the uniqueness check to the admin's organization by adding `erp_hrm_organization_id: props.admin.organizationId` to the findFirst where clause. This is both a logic flaw and a potential information disclosure (reveals existence of role names in other orgs).

issues.description: The invitation cancellation provider does not verify that the authenticated member has the authority (e.g., org:manage permission or owner/manager role) to cancel invitations. Any member of the organization can cancel any pending invitation.
issues.suggestion: Check that the member has the appropriate permission (e.g., org:manage) before allowing invitation cancellation. Query the member's role permissions and throw 403 if insufficient.

issues.description: The contract listing provider queries contracts by employeeId without verifying that the employee belongs to the admin's organization. An admin could enumerate contracts of employees from other organizations.
issues.suggestion: Join or filter by organization: add a nested where condition to verify the employee's organization matches the admin's context, e.g., `employee: { erp_hrm_organization_id: props.admin.organizationId }` in the where clause.

issues.description: All controller methods use `console.log(error)` for error handling. This is the standard AutoBE pattern but provides unstructured logging with no severity levels, correlation IDs, or audit trail.
issues.suggestion: Consider adopting a structured logging library (e.g., Winston, Pino) with log levels and request correlation IDs for production observability.

issues.description: No rate limiting is applied to any of the project management endpoints (create, list, get, update, delete).
issues.suggestion: Apply rate limiting at the API gateway or using NestJS ThrottlerModule to prevent abuse of these endpoints.

issues.description: No ownership/membership check is performed to verify that the authenticated member belongs to the requested organizationId. Any authenticated member can query invitations for any organization by supplying an arbitrary organizationId.
issues.suggestion: Before executing the query, verify that props.member.id is an active employee/member of props.organizationId. E.g., query erp_hrm_employees where erp_hrm_member_id = props.member.id AND erp_hrm_organization_id = props.organizationId and throw 403 if not found.

issues.description: The invitation lookup does not filter by the admin's organization. An admin from organization A could resend an invitation belonging to organization B by guessing its UUID.
issues.suggestion: Add a where clause condition: erp_hrm_organization_id: props.admin.organizationId (or equivalent field from AdminPayload) to scope the lookup to the admin's own organization.

issues.description: The report is fetched by ID without verifying it belongs to the admin's organization. An admin could create parameters for a report in a different organization.
issues.suggestion: Add erp_hrm_organization_id: props.admin.organizationId (or equivalent) to the findUniqueOrThrow where clause to ensure the report belongs to the admin's organization.

issues.description: The project is fetched by ID without verifying it belongs to the admin's organization. An admin could list tasks for projects in other organizations.
issues.suggestion: Add erp_hrm_organization_id: props.admin.organizationId to the findUniqueOrThrow where clause for the project lookup.

issues.description: The invitation token is looked up using findFirst with only token and deleted_at filters. If tokens are UUIDs (v4), the entropy is adequate, but there is no expiry check at the database query level and no token length/format validation before the DB query, meaning any string value is passed directly to the database lookup.
issues.suggestion: Validate that props.token matches UUID format before querying the database. Consider also adding an index on the token column and ensuring tokens are invalidated (deleted or status changed) after use to prevent replay.

issues.description: The raw JWT access and refresh tokens are stored in plaintext in the erp_hrm_member_sessions table (access_token and refresh_token columns). If the database is compromised, all active tokens are immediately usable by an attacker.
issues.suggestion: Store only a hash (e.g., SHA-256) of the tokens in the database. On validation, hash the presented token and compare against the stored hash.

issues.description: Same as postErpHrmAuthGuestJoin: raw JWT access and refresh tokens are stored in plaintext in erp_hrm_member_sessions.
issues.suggestion: Store only a cryptographic hash of the tokens in the session table.

issues.description: No password complexity validation is enforced before hashing. Any string, including a single character, is accepted as a valid password.
issues.suggestion: Add minimum length and complexity requirements (e.g., minimum 8 characters, mixed case, digit) using typia tags or a validation pipe before calling PasswordUtil.hash.

issues.description: Registration and authentication events (successful join, failed attempts) are not logged for audit purposes. This applies to postErpHrmAuthGuestJoin and postErpHrmAuthMemberJoin.
issues.suggestion: Add structured audit logging for registration events including IP address, timestamp, and outcome.

issues.description: No rate limiting is applied to the guest join or member join endpoints, making them susceptible to account enumeration and brute-force registration attacks.
issues.suggestion: Apply rate limiting at the infrastructure or middleware level (e.g., NestJS ThrottlerModule) on authentication and registration endpoints.

agentEvaluations.summary: [sampled 30/186 files] [3 chunks, 24→24 issues] The codebase shows a generally sound security architecture with proper use of MemberAuth and AdminAuth decorators on sensitive endpoints (timers, timelogs, admin operations). The main concern is the ErphrmMembersController which exposes member listing and profile retrieval endpoints without any authentication guard, potentially leaking PII (email, phone, avatar) to unauthenticated callers. All other controllers in this chunk properly apply authentication decorators. Standard AutoBE patterns (console.log, no rate limiting) are present but treated as suggestions per guidelines. No injection vulnerabilities, cryptographic failures, or SSRF issues were identified in this chunk.

issues.description: Importing from non-existent package '@ORGANIZATION/PROJECT-api'. This appears to be a placeholder that wasn't replaced with actual package name.
issues.suggestion: Replace '@ORGANIZATION/PROJECT-api' with the actual package name or remove these imports if they're not used.

issues.description: Multiple imports are declared but never used in the function body: ArrayUtil, jwt, typia, v4, PasswordUtil, toISOStringSafe, and many interface imports.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports to clean up the code.

issues.description: The timer query uses `findUnique` with a non-unique field `erp_hrm_employee_id`. According to requirements, an employee can have only one active timer at a time, but this should be enforced by a unique constraint or by querying for active status.
issues.suggestion: Change the query to find the active timer: `where: { erp_hrm_employee_id: employee.id, status: 'running' }` or similar, assuming there's a status field.

issues.description: The code uses `props.body.erpHrmProjectId` but the interface likely expects `project_id` based on naming conventions in other files. This creates inconsistency.
issues.suggestion: Check the actual interface definition and use consistent property names.

issues.description: The code validates permission codes with a regex pattern but doesn't check against the allowed set of permissions defined in other files (like putErpHrmAdminRolesRoleIdPermissionsBulk). This could allow invalid permissions.
issues.suggestion: Use the same ALLOWED_PERMISSIONS set for validation to ensure consistency.

issues.description: Property name inconsistency: uses `erpHrmProjectId` (camelCase with prefix) while other files use `project_id` (snake_case).
issues.suggestion: Standardize property names according to the interface definitions.

issues.description: Importing from '@prisma/sdk' is incorrect. The correct Prisma client import should be '@prisma/client'.

lines.text: * Creates a new guest account and establishes an authenticated session. This endpoint handles user registration by accepting email, password, and organization details. The email field must be a valid unique email address not already registered in erp_hrm_members table. The password field must meet minimum security requirements (minimum 8 characters). The password_confirmation field must exactly match the password field. The organization_name field is required and sets up the user's initial organization. The currency field defaults to USD if not provided. The timezone field defaults to UTC if not provided. Upon successful registration, the system creates the member account in erp_hrm_members, the organization in erp_hrm_organizations, and the employee record in erp_hrm_employees with owner role. The response contains JWT access_token and refresh_token for authenticated sessions.

lines.text: * Creates a new administrator account for organization management. Accepts email (used as unique identifier), password (bcrypt hashed), and display name. items.message: Class "ErphrmAuthMemberController" missing JSDoc

lines.text: * Registers a new member account in the system. Accepts email and password credentials along with display name for the new account. The email must be unique across all member accounts in the system. Upon successful registration, a new member record is created in the erp_hrm_members table with the provided email, bcrypt-hashed password stored in password_hash field, and display_name. An email verification token is generated and stored in erp_hrm_member_email_verifications with an expiration timestamp. JWT access and refresh tokens are returned to the client for immediate authentication. The member can then use the returned tokens to access protected endpoints. If the provided email already exists in the erp_hrm_members table, the operation returns a conflict error.
